summary: Health Rules UI frontend testing complete. All 16 test cases passed (100%). The Operator Rule Management UI at /operator/health-rules is fully functional with all required features working correctly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Health Rules UI is fully functional. All features verified: navigation, table display, toggle switches with optimistic UI, edit modal with all form elements (enabled switch, severity dropdown, cooldown input, threshold JSON textarea), validation for invalid JSON and cooldown < 1, save/cancel buttons, Guardian access denial.
verified_features: Sidebar has 'Health Rules' nav item with data-testid='op-nav-health-rules' - PASS
Clicking Health Rules nav navigates to /operator/health-rules - PASS
Health Rules page shows data-testid='op-health-rules-page' - PASS
Table shows all 3 rules: Low Battery, Reboot Anomaly, Signal Degradation - PASS
Each rule row has: rule name, toggle switch, severity badge, cooldown, threshold summary, edit button - PASS
Toggle switch works with optimistic UI update - PASS
Severity badges: low=gray/slate, medium=amber (colors verified) - PASS
Threshold summary is human-readable (e.g. 'Battery < 20% (recover +5%), sustain 10m') - PASS
Edit button opens modal with data-testid='rule-edit-modal' - PASS
Edit modal contains: enabled switch, severity dropdown (low/medium/high), cooldown input, threshold JSON textarea - PASS
Saving with invalid JSON shows 'Invalid JSON' error and disables save button - PASS
Saving with cooldown < 1 shows 'Must be at least 1 minute' error and disables save button - PASS
Cancel button closes modal without saving - PASS
Save button enabled with valid data - PASS
Severity dropdown shows Low, Medium, High options - PASS
Guardian user cannot access /operator/health-rules (redirected to /family, shows 'Access denied. Operator role required.') - PASS
